headline: West Midlands Creates 100 Nest Boxes for Endangered Swifts
meta_description: The West Midlands is installing 100 new nest boxes to help endangered swifts, migratory birds that depend on buildings for nesting habitat.
summary_short: The West Midlands is adding 100 nest boxes to help endangered swifts find safe breeding places in their urban environment.
body:
Swifts are remarkable birds—they spend most of their lives in flight, returning to buildings only to breed and nest. Yet their populations have been declining as modern architecture and renovation practices have eliminated the nooks and crevices they rely on to raise their young.

Recognizing this threat, the West Midlands has launched a conservation effort by installing 100 new nest boxes. These purpose-built structures provide the safe, sheltered spaces that swifts desperately need during breeding season. The initiative combines urban development with wildlife protection, proving that cities and nature can thrive together.

As a migratory species, swifts connect different parts of the world. Protecting their breeding habitat in the West Midlands supports not just local ecology but the survival of these remarkable travelers. The project shows how thoughtful urban planning can restore habitat for endangered birds while enriching the community's natural heritage.
